zh.lifeSummary: Thomas Staunton是Hugh Staunton的兒子，於1374年出生在英國艾可。他於大約1401年與Elizabeth Merdeley在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾史道頓結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1446年4月在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾史道頓享年72歲。

nameMeanings.name: Staunton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Caitlin, Ciaran, Cormac.

English and Irish: variant of Stanton . This English name has been established (in this spelling) since medieval times in Ireland (Mayo, Connacht), where it has been Gaelicized as de Stonndún. Compare McEvilly .

variants.name: McEvilly
meanings: Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac an Mhílidh ‘son of the knight’, from Gaelic míleadh ‘warrior, knight’. In County Mayo and County Cork, a family of English origin Gaelicized their name from Staunton to Mac an Mhílidh, apparently because of the latter's presumed similarity to the name of their ancestor, Milo de Stanton.

variants.name: Stanton
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places in England called Stanton or Staunton, especially Stanton in Staffordshire and Staunton in Worcestershire, all named with Old English stān ‘stone’ + tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Most of the placenames come from their situation on stony ground, but in the case of Stanton Harcourt in Oxfordshire and Stanton Drew in Avon the reference is to the proximity of prehistoric stone monuments. The name has also sometimes been chosen by Ashkenazic Jews as an Americanized form of various like-sounding Jewish surnames. This surname has also long been established in Ireland (especially Mayo).

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Staunton was born in 1374, in Essex, England as the son of Hugh Staunton. He married Elizabeth Merdeley about 1401, in Staunton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died in April 1446, in Staunton, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 72.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Stauntonは1374年にイギリスエセックスでHugh Stauntonの息子として生まれた。約1401年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアスタウントンでElizabeth Merdeleyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1446年4月にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアスタウントンで72歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Staunton(은)는 1374년 영국 에세스에서 Hugh Staunton의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 대략 1401년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 스타운턴에서 Elizabeth Merdeley(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1446년 4월 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 스타운턴에서 72세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas Staunton родился в 1374 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. Его отца звали Hugh Staunton. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Merdeley около 1401 г. в местности стаунтон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер апрель 1446 г. в местности стаунтон, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 72 лет.
